channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Esports Nations Cup: PUBG Rosters and Invited Teams Confirmed
item.content:encoded:
The Esports Foundation has revealed the official team rosters and invites for the PUBG portion of the upcoming international event, poised to take place in November in Saudi Arabia. It’s one of the most ambitious esports tournaments in history, and it’s set to identify which country is truly the best when it comes to competitive gaming.

It was confirmed that a whopping 80 rosters representing as many countries submitted their application for the PUBG tournament at the Esports Nations Cup 2026, but only 24 squads will make it to the final at the ENC.

All Players Invited to PUBG at the Esports Nations Cup

The Esports Nations Cup will follow the Esports World Cup this year, the latter of which was moved to Paris just weeks ago. It has proven to be a shrewd relocation, as the fans are gathering and the ceremonies are shaping up quite nicely.

In a new press release, the Esports Foundation revealed the spread of PUBG rosters competing at the Esports Nations Cup this coming November:

  1. THAILAND – Nourinz, Lericz, Scappy, TiGGER, Belmoth, KiSS
  2. PEOPLE’S REPUBLIC OF CHINA – MMing, 04NB, LilGhost, tiantian, HS
  3. REPUBLIC OF KOREA – EENd, Heather, Heaven, Gyumin, seoul, Seongjang
  4. VIETNAM – Himass, Delwyn, TanVuu, YmCuD, Zest, DjChip
  5. UKRAINE – Feyerist, Hakatory, SoseD, DiFX, Hexecu1or, Drugomu
  6. UNITED STATES OF AMERICA – Purdy, Shrimzy, Kickstart, CowBoi, diyy, SneakAttack
  7. CROATIA – Lukarux, NIXZYEE, slqven, Nod1, ivas, ZEKO
  8. DENMARK – Beami, Gustav, Ketter, Mikzenn, Doffy, BAAAD3
  9. FINLAND – curexi, SkaV, S4TTA, iExE, stratos, Mise
  10. BRAZIL – zkrakenN, sparkingg, possa, cauan7zin, Tny7, fana
  11. AUSTRALIA – TGLTN, luke12, Fludd, Monty, Ripp, Dusky
  12. ARGENTINA – Caspitan, emikxz, PIPAA, Dr4FTk1NG, Kalnixx, SzylzEN

These are the twelve invited teams that have earned those qualifications by placing high in tournaments in official PUBG esports events around the world. Most of the competitors invited were on the stage during the recent PUBG Nations Cup, a similarly named tournament that pulled in record viewership figures.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

The PUBG portion of the Esports Nations Cup is expected to be one of the most energetic tournaments during the entire showcase. Are you looking forward to it? Let us know your predictions on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that the COD Champs bracket is locked in, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

item.title: Subnautica CEO Resigns as Krafton Agrees to Pay ‘Significant’ Compensation to Entire Staff
item.content:encoded:
In an all-new report, it has been confirmed that Krafton has agreed to pay extensive bonuses to the staff at Unknown Worlds Entertainment, bringing an end to a months-long financial dispute. The legal issues involved a lawsuit filed against the studio’s leadership team, based on claims that they’d ‘abandoned’ their management duties.

The situation stemmed from Krafton’s original $500 million purchase of Unknown Worlds, which took place in 2021. There was an additional $250 million promised in bonuses to the company if Subnautica 2, then in development, was a success.

Today, it has been confirmed that Krafton has finally settled on that payout, and Unknown Worlds’ CEO, Ted Gill, has resigned.

Unknown Worlds Entertainment Situation is Settled

Bloomberg’s Jason Schreier confirmed that Ted Gill, CEO at Unknown Worlds, the studio behind Subnautica, has resigned. Last year, Gill was dismissed by Krafton as the financial and legal concerns escalated. The situation became even more convoluted when Gill was reinstated as the CEO in March 2026.

Speaking to Bloomberg, Gill explained his departure:

We mutually agreed to part ways. New leadership is the best way for the studio to move forward.

Gill also reported that Krafton’s payout to the staff of Unknown Worlds Entertainment will be ‘significantly more’ than originally intended, and that staff will receive ‘further incentives’ as they keep working on Subnautica 2.

Subnautica 2 was released in early access on May 14th, and it quickly soared up the charts with unimaginable power. On Steam alone, it hit almost half a million concurrent players within a matter of hours, racking up tens of thousands of positive reviews.

Per the details published by Bloomberg, every employee at Unknown Worlds will receive a portion of the bonus, including staff who might have only joined recently.

  • RELATED: What Will Be Improved in Subnautica 2

Now that this issue seems to have been put to bed, it effectively brings to a close several months of litigation and drama.

Did you give Subnautica 2 a try yet? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

item.title: Undead Chronicles Will Be “Miserable, Creepy, and Depressive”, and That’s Great
item.content:encoded:
Undead Chronicles is a ‘slow-burn horror FPS’ that’s gathering traction on Steam. It’s approaching 100,000 wishlist spots, has a demo landing this October, and is shaping up nicely in preview materials. It has been on my radar for a hot minute on account of the way it’s approaching the concept of a zombie apocalypse.

It’s not a run-and-gun action title, nor is it a run-of-the-mill survival shooter. It’s a brutal, realistic, and weighted end-of-the-world FPS that encourages you to shoot straight, watch your back, and accept that there is absolutely no mercy to be found from the undead.

I recently caught up with the game’s creator, Seyfettin Dincturk, to learn more about Undead Chronicles.

The Inspiration Behind Undead Chronicles

In a post on social media, Seyfettin Dincturk, the creator of Undead Chronicles, explained that the game is going to be ‘miserable, creepy, and depressive’. It’s going for a darker vibe than most zombie shooters accommodate these days, feeding more into the ‘Romero’ shamblers that popularized the concept of the undead several decades ago.

Undead Chronicles started life four years ago, taking shape as a pixel art game, but Dincturk pivoted from Unity to Unreal Engine 5, rebuilding the game as a quality first-person shooter.

Today, Undead Chronicles is being pieced together by a very small team that’s ‘passionate about single-player games.’ It has grown organically, and it’s fast becoming one of the most-wanted horror shooters coming to Steam.

I asked Dincturk what inspired Undead Chronicles:

We really love the vibe of (The Last of Us), Bloodborne, Resident Evil, and ZombiU…

Most zombie games involve too much comedy to compensate for very long completion hours and to also compensate for the price tag.

I believe there is a market for a depressive story, where you are all by yourself, stuck in 1923, shooting the undead.

It’s a simple, back-to-basics premise, and that’s one of the reasons I love it. It’s just cold, hard horror, planting players in a dire situation within an eerie world and demanding that they survive with what they have to hand.

The game’s description panel on Steam helps paint a picture of the world in Undead Chronicles:

They do not explode or mutate. They behave like broken people. Some panic. Some charge. The shotgun exists for the moments when distance disappears.

Restore power by hunting fusebox pieces. Find keys hidden in jacket pockets, behind paintings, and inside desk drawers. Every drawer opens, unless it’s locked. No crowbars. No ritual candles. Just logic and observation.

There’s obviously something of a jab there at games like Resident Evil, which have replicated the same basic puzzle designs for many years. That’s not necessarily a bad thing, but it’s not the operating model that Seyfettin’s team is going for.

  • RELATED: Capcom Not Interested in Resi 5 & 6 Remakes

Undead Chronicles takes real places rooted in history and turns them upside down amid the apocalypse. The game innovatively uses shadow and darkness, with a lack of light preventing your progress, forcing you to adapt as you move. At first blush, it looks like a solid horror FPS with plenty of potential.

Do you think Undead Chronicles looks like your kind of game? Let us know your thoughts on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: First Major Windrose Content Update Detailed in New Post
item.content:encoded:
The team at Kraken Express has revealed everything you could want to know regarding the first major content update for Windrose, the open-world pirate-themed survival game that stormed up the charts just a few months ago. The game was released into early access on April 14, and it has carved out a nice space for itself in the survival niche thus far.

In the post on Steam, the team at Kraken uncovered everything coming in the Ashlands update, including the expansion of the story, new ships, and improvements to the game’s core operating model.

Windrose ‘Ashlands’ Update Explained by Devs

To open the blog post on Steam, the team at Kraken Express explained that Windrose’s story will be getting a sizeable buff when the Ashlands update drops. New factions will be introduced, and both the main story and side quests will be expanded nicely. They’re taking on the task of amending the existing factions too, making them more detailed and unique.

With the Ashlands update comes the Ashlands biome, a new region in the game, pitted and scarred by volcanic eruptions. It’s a dangerous place to explore, but it’s bursting with high-tier loot and plenty of resources for those who dare tread there.

Kraken is exploring new ways to expand the concept of base-building in Windrose, stressing that they’ve been inspired by what the community has managed to create thus far. They want to foster that creativity and let it bloom, so watch this space.

The team then admitted a weak spot that’s being addressed:

We feel that our ship gameplay has a lot of room for expansion, and we also have tons of your ideas and requests to draw from. It won’t be possible to tuck everything we’d like into one update, but we’ll surely experiment with and implement a few additions to this part of Windrose.

With that said, they’re trying to enrich and deepen ship gameplay, add more ship classes, and give combat gameplay ‘more care and attention’. There are also improvements inbound for NPC ships, which should reflect in more natural behaviour.

Are you still playing Windrose? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the new screenshots from the ‘Last of Us’ multiplayer game, and for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter
